claims.claim: A bento grid requires exactly one 2×2 anchor cell to establish visual hierarchy; without it, all cells read as equal weight.
claims.quote: exactly one 2x2 anchor cell creates the hierarchy via grid-column: span 2; grid-row: span 2

claims.claim: grid-auto-flow: dense fills layout holes left by spanning cells but decouples visual order from DOM order, causing WCAG 2.4.3 focus-order violations that screen readers and keyboard users encounter.
claims.quote: grid-auto-flow: dense and span juggling reorder cells visually while screen readers and keyboard focus follow DOM order, violating WCAG 2.4.3

claims.claim: Border radius below 16px causes a bento grid to visually revert to a plain card grid, making the threshold between 20–28px functionally important for the aesthetic.
claims.quote: under 16px it reads as a plain card grid

claims.claim: A 4-column bento grid collapses on mobile to 8–12 stacked cards with no hierarchy, making it unsuitable for mobile-heavy traffic.
claims.quote: A 4-column bento collapses to 8 to 12 stacked cards, which is just a long, hierarchy-free scroll

claims.claim: CSS Grid with grid-auto-flow: dense has had full cross-browser support since 2017 and requires no vendor prefixes as of 2026.
claims.quote: CSS Grid and grid-auto-flow: dense have shipped in every major browser since 2017 with no prefixes needed in 2026
